mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month increase in nonfarm payrolls indicates expanding labor income and consumer spending capacity, which drives corporate earnings expectations higher and produces positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 forward 20-day returns are not significantly positive in months where PAYEMS increases relative to months where it decreases (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A federal funds rate above 3.5% restricts credit availability and raises discount rates applied to corporate cash flows, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over the following 20 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 20-day returns are positive in more than 50% of months when FEDFUNDS exceeds 3.5%.

mechanism_narrative: A high federal funds rate combined with a low CPIAUCSL level below 340 signals a restrictive real monetary stance that chokes nominal demand and corporate pricing power, generating negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
regime_hint: restrictive monetary policy into low inflation
failure_condition: A regression of 60-day returns on FEDFUNDS, CPIAUCSL, and their interaction term yields an interaction coefficient that is not negative and statistically significant at the 5% level.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate is already below 5%, a further month-over-month decline signals an overheating labor market that accelerates wage-push inflation and impending monetary tightening, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
regime_hint: late-cycle expansion with labor market tightness
failure_condition: S&P 500 20-day returns are positive in more than 55% of triggered instances when UNRATE is below 5% and declines by more than 0.1 percentage points.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month increase in retail sales reflects robust consumer expenditure and top-line revenue growth for consumer-facing corporations, which drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Month-over-month increases in RSAFS do not coincide with positive 20-day S&P 500 returns in more than 50% of observations.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production exceeding the 100 index threshold signals above-trend manufacturing utilization and capacity expansion, which lifts corporate earnings expectations and generates positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 60-day returns are negative in more than 50% of instances when INDPRO exceeds 100.

mechanism_narrative: Elevated housing starts above 1400 thousand units coinciding with an unemployment rate below 5% indicate a synchronised expansion in construction activity and household labor income, amplifying aggregate demand and producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: A linear model with an interaction term between HOUST and UNRATE does not produce a positive and statistically significant coefficient (p < 0.05) in a regression of 20-day S&P 500 returns.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month increase in the federal funds rate reprices short-term discount rates and credit risk premia upward, causing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Negative 5-day S&P 500 returns occur in fewer than 55% of months when FEDFUNDS increases month-over-month.
